key_insight: GNDEs can be trained on a smaller sparse random graph and then deployed zero-shot on larger graphs, with theory guaranteeing convergence to graphon limits.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled size-transfer framework for scalable graph dynamics models, reducing retraining costs when graph size changes.
method: Define Graphon Neural Differential Equations and their adjoint systems as infinite-node limits of GNDEs.
Prove trajectory and adjoint convergence on sparse graphon-sampled graphs with rates depending on sparsity and graph size.
Analyze both DTO and OTD training under explicit Euler discretization to quantify optimization/discretization error.
evidence_signal: Experiments on HSBM and tent graphons, plus zero-shot transfer across four graphon classes, show accurate deployment on larger independently sampled graphs.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on sparse random graph assumptions, graphon sampling, and discretization/sparsity regimes, so performance may degrade outside these conditions.
paper_title: Zero-Shot Size Transfer for Neural ODEs on Sparse Random Graphs: Graphon Limits and Adjoint Convergence
